verses.text: The LORD: let Israel keep the Passover at its appointed season — the fourteenth day of the first month at evening, in the wilderness of Sinai. Israel kept the Passover according to all the LORD commanded. Guidance: One year after the exodus — Israel keeps Passover again, on Sinai's edge. The same date, the same lamb. God's people learn to make memorial a habit, not just a moment.

Certain men were defiled by a dead body and could not keep the Passover that day. They came before Moses: why are we kept back from offering at the LORD's appointed time? Moses: wait, and I will hear what the LORD commands concerning you. Guidance: Men who wanted to worship but couldn't. Their grief over being excluded becomes a prayer that opens a new law. God listens to the longing of those who feel kept out.

The LORD: if defiled by a dead body or on a journey afar — keep the Passover the fourteenth day of the second month at evening, with unleavened bread and bitter herbs. No bone broken. If clean and forbears — that soul cut off. The stranger shall keep it — one law. Guidance: Second-chance Passover. Defilement and distance get a do-over a month later. But choosing not to remember when you could — that is cut off. God provides for the truly hindered, but not for the merely indifferent.

On the day the tabernacle was reared, the cloud covered it; at evening as fire till morning. When the cloud rose, Israel journeyed; where it stayed, they encamped. Two days, a month, a year — they kept the LORD's charge. At His commandment they rested and journeyed. Guidance: Israel moves only when God moves. Cloud by day, fire by night — His presence sets the pace. Some seasons stretch into a year; some last two days. Faith learns to read the weather and obey.
